meta.title: MCP Agent Workflow
meta.description: 3-lane swimlane: AI AGENT (top) calls 7 MCP tools, COMMAND CENTER (middle) authenticates and dispatches each call, CLOUDNODE (bottom) produces physical data when needed. v1.1: agent-lane dots are now classification-coloured (READ green / VISUAL amber / WRITE purple) so the bottom legend actually maps to what's drawn — the v1.0 render coloured all 7 agent dots uniformly purple, which made the legend's READ/VISUAL/WRITE colour key orphan content.

canvas.style_notes: Flat dark UI. Three horizontal swimlanes with subtle dashed coloured rails. Numbered step circles on each lane. NOT photographic, NOT 3D, NOT illustrative.

visual.description: returns images the model can see

step_rendering.agent_lane_dot: FILLED circle whose colour comes from the step's classification: READ steps render in green (#22c55e), VISUAL steps render in amber (#f59e0b), WRITE steps render in purple (#a855f7). White step number (1–7) inside each dot. Thin matching-coloured outer ring at ~18% alpha. This is the visual axis the bottom legend describes — DO NOT colour all agent dots the same.
step_rendering.command_center_lane_dot: Hollow / outlined-only circle with a thin white border at ~35% alpha and a white step number (1–7) inside on a transparent fill. Deliberately neutral so it reads as 'CC dispatched this call' without competing with the legend's READ-green colour key (which now belongs to the agent-lane dot for step 1).
step_rendering.cloudnode_lane_dot: blue (#3b82f6) filled circle with a white check mark (✓) — only appears when node_present is true (steps 2, 3, 5, 6).
step_rendering.tool_name_label: above each agent dot in JetBrains Mono monospace, muted grey (#9ca3af).
step_rendering.node_outcome_label: below the cloudnode lane in Inter sans-serif, muted grey — always shown even when the cloudnode dot is absent.
step_rendering.vertical_connecting_lines: thin dashed white at 5% opacity running top to bottom through each step column.

items.trailing_text: returns images the model can see

legend_panel.intent: The three bullet colours in the legend MUST match the three filled-circle colours actually used on the agent lane. Step 1 (list_cameras) is the only green dot, steps 2-3 (view_camera, watch_camera) are the amber dots, steps 4-7 (create_incident, attach_snapshot, attach_clip, finalize_incident) are the purple dots.

exact_text: returns images the model can see
constraints.do_not_render_metadata: Do NOT render any JSON keys, position values, hex colour codes, rgba() values, x_pct/y_pct positioning values, font-weight numbers, letter-spacing values, percentage alpha values, or any other JSON metadata as visible text on the canvas. The ONLY visible text on the canvas must come from the exact_text array — lane labels, tool names, outcome labels, the numbered dot digits 1-7, and the legend (READ / VISUAL / WRITE plus their description lines). No other text whatsoever.
constraints.do_not_uniformly_colour_agent_dots: Critical: the seven agent-lane dots must NOT all share the same colour. Their colours are driven per-step by the classification field — green for step 1 only, amber for steps 2-3, purple for steps 4-7. Uniform colouring (e.g. all purple, as in v1.0) makes the bottom legend orphan content and is what this v1.1 spec exists to fix.
constraints.rendering_notes: Premium technical-doc style. Crisp typography. Tool names in monospace, lane labels in spaced caps. Numbered dots are EXACTLY 1-7 on both the agent and CC lanes. The CLOUDNODE lane shows ONLY check-mark dots, and only at columns 2, 3, 5, 6 (view_camera, watch_camera, attach_snapshot, attach_clip). NOT photographic. Aspect ratio 16:9. The canvas should look like a polished product diagram, not a debug view of a JSON spec — no JSON keys, no position values, no font/spacing parameters as visible text.
